points: Stars are celestial bodies that shine with their own light. Constellations are 88 officially recognized regions in the sky containing star patterns.
Crucial patterns include Orion (Hunter), Ursa Major (Saptarishi/Big Dipper), and Ursa Minor (Little Dipper).
The Pole Star, found using the Big Dipper, appears stationary in the North and is vital for navigation in the Northern hemisphere.
The Solar System comprises the Sun, eight planets, moons, asteroids, and comets.
The Sun is our closest star and the main energy source, while planets (Inner rocky and Outer gas giants) revolve around it.
Venus is the brightest planet, Mars is reddish, and Earth is the 'Blue Planet' due to its water surface.
Natural satellites (Moons) revolve around planets; Earth has one moon that takes 27 days for one revolution.
India's Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the Moon's south pole on Aug 23 (National Space Day).
Asteroids are rocky objects between Mars and Jupiter, while Comets are icy-rocky visitors from outer regions with long tails.
The Milky Way Galaxy (Akasha Ganga) is our home galaxy, containing billions of stars including the Sun.
Our galaxy is just one of many billions that make up the vast Universe.
India's Hanle Observatory on Mount Saraswati promotes astronomy and is part of the Hanle Dark Sky Reserve.
